Risk Flags
2nd Floor Bath Ratio — Confirm Before Signing
Kubi Casa shows 1 bath on 2nd floor. If Primary (Room 5) has en-suite (4th bath), Rooms 6–9 share 1 bath = 4:1. Your rule: max 3 per bath. Options: (A) add 2nd bath upstairs in rehab (~$5–8K — revise budget); (B) drop to 3 rooms on 2nd = 7 total rooms; (C) accept 4:1 and price Rooms 6–9 slightly lower. Confirm on walkthrough.
Parking Capacity — ~5 Spots Needed
60% of 9 tenants = ~5–6 cars. Property shows driveway + attached garage (right side). Typical JAX driveway = 2–3 cars; garage = 1–2 cars = 4–5 total. May be 1 short. Confirm on walkthrough. If tight: consider converting garage to parking only (not storage). Fallback: 8 rooms if parking is a hard constraint.
Cross-Collateral Release — Must Be in Writing
7862 Hastings pledged as security. Contract must include: explicit release trigger (balloon refi or payoff), no cross-default clause (Skycrest default does not trigger Hastings default), and no prepayment penalty. Have title attorney review before signing.
Electric Bill — Monitor After Move-In
$600/mo is the estimate for a fully occupied 3,000+ sqft all-electric house in JAX. Actual bills vary by season (AC-heavy summers). First 90 days: track monthly and adjust OpEx model accordingly. If bill trends above $700, that affects CF by ~$100/mo and should be factored into pricing strategy.
Raj = Private Lender — Relationship Asset
Not a distressed seller. Raj is a sophisticated private lender who wants reliable yield ($100K interest over 5 years) and maintained collateral. His priorities: (1) payment on time, (2) property condition, (3) clean exit. Monthly performance statements build trust toward an extension request or next deal. Treat as a lender relationship, not a transaction.
Dining Area Conversion — Straightforward
The dining area (Room 4) is already a defined space off the kitchen. A simple door addition and portable closet converts it without load-bearing wall work. Family Room (Room 3) is even easier — it's a standalone space with its own entry. Both conversions are within the $15K rehab budget assuming no structural work is needed.

Closing Notes — What to Lock In with Raj at Signing

1
Confirm the 5% IO Structure
"Raj, you mentioned 5% interest-only — that puts my payment at $1,667/mo on the $400K. Is that what you're thinking?"
He named it. Don't negotiate against yourself. Lock it at 5%.
2
Lock in the 5+5 Extension Option
"I'd like to include an option to extend for another 5 years at balloon — you've got a performing property as collateral, so it's low risk for you. Can we build that in?"
Get this in writing at signing. Year 5 DSCR qualifies at 1.91× anyway — you're not trapped even without the extension.
3
Cross-Collateral Release + No Cross-Default
"On the Hastings cross-collateral — once I pay the balloon or refi, Hastings releases automatically, correct? And there's no cross-default — a problem on Skycrest doesn't touch Hastings?"
Critical. Don't sign without both clauses confirmed in writing.
4
No Prepayment Penalty
"No prepayment penalty if I want to pay it off early or refi before Year 5?"
Private lenders usually accommodate. Just confirm so there's no surprise at Year 3 if you want to refinance early.
5
Set Monthly Communication Expectation
"I'll send you a monthly statement — payment, occupancy, quick property note. You'll never have to wonder what's going on with your collateral."
Raj is a lender. Monthly touchpoints = trust = easier extension or next deal together.

Open Items Before Go / No-Go

1
Confirm 4th bath location on walkthrough
Primary en-suite vs 2nd shared bath determines upstairs room count and rehab budget. Decision changes CoC slightly.
2
Pull DealMachine ARV + 32246 PadSplit active comps
ARV currently $500K (conservative — largest house in neighborhood by ~2x, comps will run smaller). Validate via DealMachine API when key is stored in keychain ("dealmachine-api"). Run 9 rooms through PadSplit calculator (link below) to cross-check active 32246 rates.
3
Count parking spots on walkthrough
Driveway + garage = ~4–5 spots. Need 5–6 for 9 rooms at 60% rule. Affects room count decision.
4
Attorney review — cross-collateral, release, no cross-default, extension clause
Title attorney must confirm all four before signing. Non-negotiable.
